title: Fix actor initialization and deposit network connectivity errors

requirements.summary: Fix 'Actor not available' error in WalletDeposit.tsx by ensuring backend actor is fully initialized before deposit operations
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Actor not available' error no longer appears when users access the deposit page
Actor initialization is verified before rendering deposit form components
Loading state is displayed while actor initializes
User-friendly error message is shown if actor fails to initialize

file_operations.description: Add actor availability checks before rendering deposit form. Display loading state while actor initializes using the isFetching state from useActor hook. Show user-friendly error message if actor initialization fails. Prevent premature access to actor instance by conditionally rendering deposit form only when actor is confirmed available and not in fetching state.

requirements.summary: Resolve deposit network connectivity issues by improving actor connection sequence validation
acceptanceCriteria: Users can successfully submit deposit requests without network errors
Connection state is properly validated before deposit operations
Network failures trigger automatic retry logic with exponential backoff
Clear error messages guide users when network issues occur

file_operations.description: Enhance deposit submission flow to validate connection state before calling backend methods. Verify actor instance exists and authentication state is valid before allowing deposit submission. Improve integration with existing exponential backoff retry logic in useRetryConnection hook. Add clear error messaging for different network failure scenarios (timeout, canister unreachable, authentication issues) using the errorMessages utility.

requirements.summary: Add defensive checks in deposit submission flow to verify actor instance exists before calling backend methods
acceptanceCriteria: All backend actor method calls are protected with existence checks
Try-catch blocks capture and handle actor-related errors gracefully
Users see specific error messages for different failure scenarios
Failed operations can be retried without refreshing the page

file_operations.description: Wrap all actor method calls in try-catch blocks with explicit null/undefined checks before invocation. Add specific error handling for actor availability issues separate from other network errors. Implement retry capability that allows users to resubmit failed deposit operations without page refresh by resetting error state and enabling the submit button again. Use structured error messages from errorMessages utility to provide actionable guidance.

requirements.summary: Enhance useActor hook to ensure stable initialized actor instance with proper error state management
acceptanceCriteria: useActor hook validates actor readiness before returning instance
Hook provides clear error states when actor initialization fails
Consuming components can reliably detect when actor is unavailable
Actor instance remains stable across component re-renders

file_operations.description: Update query hooks that depend on actor availability to properly handle actor initialization states. Add enabled conditions that check both actor existence and isFetching state from useActor. Ensure queries don't execute prematurely before actor is ready. Return loading states that accurately reflect actor dependency so consuming components can distinguish between actor initialization and data fetching.

requirements.summary: Add comprehensive error logging to capture actor initialization and deposit submission flow
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs capture all critical steps in the actor initialization sequence
Error logs include stack traces and relevant state information
Network request failures are logged with response details
Logs help identify whether issues are authentication, network, or actor-related

file_operations.description: Add detailed console logging throughout the deposit flow. Log actor initialization status when component mounts. Log authentication state and identity verification steps. Capture all exception details including stack traces, error types, and relevant component state (actor availability, authentication status, connection state). Log network request attempts and responses. Include timestamps and contextual information to help diagnose whether failures originate from actor initialization, authentication, or network connectivity.
